capabilities: Combine stateful event stream processing with AI-prompting derived knowledge bases to enhance data processing capabilities and ensure consistent, accurate results.

procedure.action: Reconcile artifacts from stateful event streams and AI-prompting by using Apache Kafka Streams to align event keys and timestamps; ensure all events are processed in order.

Assert merged output by applying JSON Schema draft-07 validation on combined data; emit VALIDATION_FAILED with field path on the first failing field by asserting all required fields conform to the schema definition; emit VALIDATION_FAILED with field path on the first failing field; assert field_validation_pass_rate >= 1.0.

Log operation duration and result codes by appending to a centralized logging system like ELK Stack; ensure logs include timestamps and unique operation IDs using domain-appropriate tooling; assert error_count < 1 and result_valid = true; log operation_duration_ms and result_code to audit trail.
Filter candidate events for processing by running a sliding window aggregation on the event stream; assert that error_count < 1 for each window.
Assign results using a pre-trained machine learning model deployed on TensorFlow Serving; ensure classification accuracy exceeds 95% on test dataset.

summary: Integrate stateful event stream processing with AI-prompting derived knowledge bases by merging workflows and validating outputs for consistency and accuracy.
title: Stateful Event Stream Processing

failureConditions: Inconsistent data when merge omits NULL handling: records with NULL key fields are silently dropped, causing referential integrity violations in downstream consumers.
High latency when event stream processing is not optimized, causing delays in data availability for AI-prompting derived knowledge base updates.
Incorrect classification results when model drift occurs, causing inaccurate insights and decisions based on outdated AI models.
metrics: merged output by applying JSON Schema draft-07 validation on combined data
